---
title: "timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-26
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [22, 8, 17, 15, 15, 23]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-10-vs-day-5 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control); columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20; horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05 and vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR). Positive log2(OR) means day-10-enriched relative to day-5 *within the same condition*; negative means day-10-depleted relative to day-5. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is implied by reading paired top/bottom rows of the grid side-by-side.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), day-10-vs-day-5 2x2 contingency per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, aa) 2x2 day-10-vs-day-5 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs (120 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher's exact; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled (day_10 + day_5) totals at the condition level are in the thousands; Fisher returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations, so `log2_OR` is the primary effect column, not p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is computed independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family", not across the full 120-row file.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition or between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, no condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the design is structurally cleaner.

---

## Headline

Moderate signal at AA-level within-condition Fisher d10 vs d5: 23/120 hits at FDR<0.05 (8/60 BWM, 15/60 control). Largest-magnitude shared-direction cells: site-A G (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.31 / control `log2_OR`=+0.39) and site-P G (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.30 / control `log2_OR`=+0.43 — control,P,G is the file minimum at p_adj=5.80e-08). Largest-magnitude divergence cells: site-E R (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.28 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.21 ns), site-A A (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.18 ns vs control `log2_OR`=+0.27), and site-P V (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.10 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.24). Cross-contrast note: site-E K (the d10_vs_d0 divergence) is null in both conditions here (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.08 ns / control `log2_OR`=-0.08 ns).

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio; positive = day-10-enriched, negative = day-10-depleted within one condition). `p_value` is the raw Fisher's exact two-sided p; `p_adj` is BH-corrected within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs. Direction is fixed by the day-10-vs-day-5 contingency layout within one condition: `log2_OR` > 0 = day-10-enriched relative to day-5 in that condition, `log2_OR` < 0 = day-10-depleted. Sub-tables below are split per-direction within each condition (4 sub-tables total, BWM/control x Enriched/Depleted). The `site` column carries the ribosome site; rows are grouped by site in canonical A / P / E order, then sorted by `p_adj` ascending with `|log2_OR|` descending as the tie-breaker. All rows with `p_adj` < 0.05 are shown; no row cap and no raw-p fallback (family-level rule for `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`). Each row's amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on every appearance.

```{=latex}
\needspace{13\baselineskip}
```

### BWM — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| amino acid | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| G (glycine) | A | 0.312 | 9.09e-05 | 9.09e-04 |  |
| R (arginine) | A | 0.222 | 0.00811 | 0.0496 |  |
| G (glycine) | P | 0.297 | 1.51e-04 | 0.00303 |  |
| G (glycine) | E | 0.279 | 0.00123 | 0.0246 |  |
| R (arginine) | E | 0.275 | 0.00279 | 0.0279 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{11\baselineskip}
```

### BWM — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| amino acid | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L (leucine) | A | -0.399 | 9.74e-06 | 1.95e-04 |  |
| Q (glutamine) | A | -0.309 | 0.00992 | 0.0496 |  |
| I (isoleucine) | E | -0.267 | 0.00468 | 0.0312 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{16\baselineskip}
```

### control — Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| amino acid | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| G (glycine) | A | 0.388 | 2.34e-07 | 3.96e-06 |  |
| P (proline) | A | 0.365 | 5.26e-04 | 0.00263 |  |
| A (alanine) | A | 0.268 | 0.00480 | 0.0166 |  |
| R (arginine) | A | 0.206 | 0.0132 | 0.0377 |  |
| G (glycine) | P | 0.430 | 2.90e-09 | 5.80e-08 |  |
| D (aspartate) | P | 0.199 | 0.00636 | 0.0232 |  |
| A (alanine) | E | 0.397 | 6.63e-05 | 0.00133 |  |
| P (proline) | E | 0.414 | 1.59e-04 | 0.00159 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{15\baselineskip}
```

### control — Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| amino acid | site | effect (`log2_OR`) | raw p (`p_value`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| N (asparagine) | A | -0.552 | 3.96e-07 | 3.96e-06 |  |
| Q (glutamine) | A | -0.534 | 1.00e-05 | 6.70e-05 |  |
| E (glutamate) | A | -0.199 | 0.00498 | 0.0166 |  |
| I (isoleucine) | P | -0.382 | 1.29e-05 | 1.29e-04 |  |
| L (leucine) | P | -0.246 | 0.00422 | 0.0211 |  |
| V (valine) | P | -0.238 | 0.00328 | 0.0211 |  |
| Q (glutamine) | P | -0.352 | 0.00695 | 0.0232 |  |

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 23 (BWM 8, control 15)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 27 (BWM 8, control 19)
- `min adjusted-p`: 3.96e-06 (tied: control,A,G and control,A,N within their family); 5.80e-08 (control,P,G, the file minimum)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the thousands has no meaningful floor; the dominant statistical-design concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide), not floor.
- Per (condition, site):
  - BWM,A: 4 sig at FDR<0.05 (G, R, L, Q); min p_adj = 1.95e-04
  - BWM,P: 1 sig (G); min p_adj = 0.00303
  - BWM,E: 3 sig (G, R, I); min p_adj = 0.0246
  - control,A: 7 sig (G, N, Q, P, A, E, R); min p_adj = 3.96e-06
  - control,P: 6 sig (G, I, L, V, D, Q); min p_adj = 5.80e-08
  - control,E: 2 sig (A, P); min p_adj = 0.00133

## Biological interpretation

**Glycine enrichment at the A and P sites is the file's largest-magnitude shared-direction signal across both conditions (concrete).** A:G and P:G move the same way in BWM and control between day 5 and day 10: BWM A:G `log2_OR`=+0.312 and P:G `log2_OR`=+0.297; control A:G `log2_OR`=+0.388 and P:G `log2_OR`=+0.430 (the file minimum). Shared-direction cells in a within-condition contrast are the design's least confounded reading: by construction, the BWM/control axis is *not* what is driving these rows, so the most parsimonious anchor is a time-axis feature of the organism itself across the d5-to-d10 window. Glycine has the most flexible backbone of the 20 amino acids and decodes via four synonymous codons (GGA/GGC/GGG/GGT); the codon-resolution sister CSV is the natural place to ask whether all four synonyms carry the signal (consistent with an amino-acid-level shift) or whether one synonym dominates (consistent with a tRNA-decoding or codon-usage shift). Whether to attach a kinetic mechanism to the A/P-Gly pattern depends on that codon decomposition.

**Three site x aa cells flip direction or move on only one side of the BWM-vs-control axis (concrete).** Site-E R is BWM-enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.275) but control-flat (`log2_OR`=-0.21, ns per Headline); site-A A is BWM-flat (`log2_OR`=-0.18, ns per Headline) but control-enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.268); site-P V is BWM-flat (`log2_OR`=+0.10, ns per Headline) but control-depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.238). E:R is the cleanest direction-flip in this contrast — both arms move, but in opposite directions. Arg at the E site sits in the basic-residue exit-tunnel-electrostatic-slowing register; a BWM-specific d5-to-d10 enrichment of E:R, set against a control side that is flat at the same cell, points at a perturbation-specific feature of basic-residue traffic through the exit channel during the second half of the timecourse. The A:A and P:V cells are different in character — control moves while BWM does not — so they read as control-side composition shifts (Ala enriching at A in control between d5 and d10; Val depleting at P in control between d5 and d10) that the BWM line did not undergo.

**Control reshuffles its site-A and site-P composition more aggressively than BWM between d5 and d10 (concrete).** Control,A carries 7 FDR<0.05 hits (G/N/Q/P/A/E/R, with the strongest cells N depleted `log2_OR`=-0.552 and Q depleted `log2_OR`=-0.534) and control,P carries 6 (G enriched / I, L, V, Q depleted / D enriched, with G the file minimum); BWM,A carries 4 (L depleted / G enriched / Q depleted / R enriched, with L and G the strongest cells and Q/R sitting at the FDR<0.05 wall) and BWM,P carries 1 (G enriched). The control panels are not flat references — they are themselves moving. So any BWM-vs-control contrast read off this file as "BWM-specific" must account for the possibility that the cell is flat in BWM only because BWM held steadier at d5-to-d10, not because of an active BWM perturbation. The largest-magnitude cells where BWM moves and control does not (E:R) carry the cleanest BWM-specific reading; cells where control moves and BWM does not (A:A, P:V) carry the cleanest control-side-drift reading.

**The d10_vs_d0 E:K direction-flip from the sister contrast does not recur here (concrete).** In the `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv` sister contrast, E:K was a direction-divergent cell with opposite-sign FDR<0.05 hits in BWM and control (specific values live in that file, not in this report). In this d10_vs_d5 file the same cell is null in both conditions (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.08 ns / control `log2_OR`=-0.08 ns, per Headline). Two readings of that pattern are consistent with the data, and this file alone cannot resolve them: (a) the d10_vs_d0 E:K flip is anchored at d0 — i.e. the perturbation moved E-site Lys traffic at d0 already, and d5-to-d10 is a plateau in both conditions; (b) the flip is carried by the d0-to-d5 step, with d5-to-d10 holding flat. The `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv` sister CSV is the deciding contrast.

**The 8-vs-15 BWM-vs-control hit-count gap is not driven by a pooled-total power asymmetry (exploratory).** BWM contributes 8/60 FDR<0.05 hits, control 15/60. The 60-vs-60 test count is balanced, and the per-condition (day_10 + day_5) marginals are also nearly balanced at the condition level: BWM day_10=6945 + day_5=11935 = 18880 pooled; control day_10=8788 + day_5=11177 = 19965 pooled (~1.06:1). This is much tighter than the d10_vs_d0 file's BWM_total ~6k vs control_total ~28k (~4.55:1) imbalance, so the d10_vs_d0 pooled-count power story does not carry over to this contrast — the same effect size would not produce systematically different p magnitudes between conditions here. The 8-vs-15 gap therefore most likely reflects an effect-size difference (consistent with paragraph 3's "control reshuffles its A and P site composition more aggressively than BWM" reading) or sampling noise on cells sitting at the FDR<0.05 wall, rather than a power asymmetry. The visual sparsity of the BWM row in the composite figure is consistent with the effect-size reading.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the A/P-Gly shared-time enrichment in both conditions (pull the codon-resolution sister CSV to ask whether the signal is amino-acid-level or driven by a synonym), the three direction-divergent cells E:R / A:A / P:V (E:R is the cleanest BWM-specific candidate; A:A and P:V are control-side composition shifts), and the d10_vs_d0 E:K flip's failure to recur here (which sets up the d5_vs_d0 aa file as the deciding contrast for whether the E:K divergence is anchored at d0 or carried by d0-to-d5). *Exploratory*: the 8-vs-15 hit-count asymmetry — per-condition d5+d10 marginals are nearly balanced (BWM 18880, control 19965; ~1.06:1), so the gap is most parsimoniously read as an effect-size signal (control reshuffles A/P composition more aggressively) rather than a power artifact; whether the residual gap survives replication is the open question. *Noise*: rare-aa rows flagged by the family-wide `rare-aa-low-count` caveat (Trp and Cys sit below count = 100 — see the `rare-aa-low-count` body caveat for the specific BWM and control counts on file; none enters Top hits in this contrast, but downstream uses of the file should not over-read magnitude on those rows — `log2_OR` magnitudes are unstable at low day_X_count and large `|log2_OR|` may reflect sampling rather than biology).

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (feature_count, total - feature_count) at each timepoint within one condition. The script (`stall_sites_non_consensus_stats.py`) computes BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 tests for AA-level (so each of the 6 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 120). The test does **not** answer whether the same feature is changing in BWM and control in the same direction — that comparison is made by reading paired panels side-by-side (see "control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction" caveat) and is the largest-magnitude reading from this file's cells. Counts (`day_X_count`) and totals (`day_X_total`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled (day_10 + day_5) totals at the condition level are in the thousands; even tiny relative deviations can drive `p_adj` to 1e-7 or below (control,P,G `log2_OR`=+0.430 is the file-minimum p_adj) — rank by `log2_OR` when ordering effects within a (condition, site) family.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; two cells with the same raw p in different (condition, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (condition, site) cell, not across the full 120-test grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file structurally answers "does day_10 differ from day_5 *within* this condition" cleanly, leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be done by reading paired panels side-by-side rather than by the test itself.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Trp (BWM W: 78 vs 108) and Cys (BWM C: 69 vs 82, control C: 61 vs 89) sit below count = 100. Neither enters Top hits at FDR<0.05 in this contrast, so no Top-hits flags carry the label here, but any downstream reading of the file should not over-read magnitude on those rows — `log2_OR` is unstable at small k. **Threshold asymmetry rationale (family-wide):** BWM_total ~6k vs control_total ~28k (~4.55:1); the family-rule 100/200 split (BWM/control) is heuristic, calibrated to catch rare AAs (W, C, M) in both arms — symmetric (100/100) under-flags control's rare cells (which sit at 150-300), proportional (100/450) over-flags. Fisher OR variance is dominated by the smallest cell ($\approx 1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d$), so absolute count — not proportion — is the relevant axis. Codon resolution stays symmetric at 50/50 because the AA-vs-codon dilution narrows the relative cell-size spread.
- **OR-direction-asymmetry.** No zero-cell rows in this file, so no infinite/zero ORs; the caveat is preserved as a ranking-axis discipline note — `log2_OR` is the reported effect column (already log-transformed), so positive/negative bands are symmetric by construction; rank directly on it.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Three site x aa cells flip direction or move on only one side: **site-E R** (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.275 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.208 ns — cleanest flip in the contrast), **site-A A** (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.179 ns vs control `log2_OR`=+0.268), **site-P V** (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.102 ns vs control `log2_OR`=-0.238). The d10_vs_d0 E:K flip does NOT recur here (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.080 ns / control `log2_OR`=-0.080 ns); cross-contrast cells provided for downstream reconciliation.
- **shared-time-trajectory-A/P-Gly.** Both BWM and control enrich Gly at sites A and P from d5-to-d10 (BWM A:G `log2_OR`=+0.312 / P:G `log2_OR`=+0.297; control A:G `log2_OR`=+0.388 / P:G `log2_OR`=+0.430, control,P,G is the file minimum). Coherent shared-time signal across both conditions — not perturbation-specific. Treat as a control-aligned time-axis feature, not a BWM-specific finding.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log2(OR) on x, -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. Leucine (L, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.40 sits highest on the y-axis (-log10(FDR) ~= 3.7) as the strongest depletion; glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= +0.31 is the strongest enrichment (-log10(FDR) ~= 3.1); arginine (R, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= +0.22 and glutamine (Q, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= -0.31 sit at the FDR=0.05 dashed line. All FDR<0.05 hits sit within the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: a near-null panel with a single FDR<0.05 hit — glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= +0.30 and -log10(FDR) ~= 2.5; all other amino acids collapse to a tight cluster near the origin below the FDR=0.05 dashed line.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: 3 FDR<0.05 hits, all sitting just above the FDR=0.05 dashed line at -log10(FDR) ~= 1.5. Isoleucine (I, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.27 (depleted); glycine (G, neutral orange) and arginine (R, basic blue) overlap at log2(OR) ~= +0.27 to +0.28 (enriched). All hits within the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band; magnitudes are small relative to the BWM,A and BWM,P signals.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control A-site amino-acid volcano: the most-populated BWM-or-control panel of the row, 7 FDR<0.05 hits. Asparagine (N, polar purple) and glycine (G, neutral orange) are the strongest cells — N at log2(OR) ~= -0.55 and G at log2(OR) ~= +0.39 both sit at -log10(FDR) ~= 5.4 (top of the panel). Glutamine (Q, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= -0.53 and proline (P, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= +0.37 sit one tier below; alanine (A, hydrophobic green) and arginine (R, basic blue) are FDR<0.05 enriched at log2(OR) ~= +0.21 to +0.27; glutamate (E, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= -0.20 is the only FDR<0.05 acidic hit in the panel.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control P-site amino-acid volcano: the file's tallest single point — glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= +0.43 sits at -log10(FDR) ~= 7.2, the file minimum. Isoleucine (I, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.38 is the strongest depletion (-log10(FDR) ~= 3.9); glutamine (Q, polar purple), leucine (L, green), and valine (V, green) form a tight FDR<0.05 depletion cluster at log2(OR) ~= -0.24 to -0.35; aspartate (D, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.20 is the lone FDR<0.05 acidic enrichment.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-5 control E-site amino-acid volcano: a near-null panel with 2 overlapping FDR<0.05 hits — alanine (A, hydrophobic green) and proline (P, neutral orange) sit together at log2(OR) ~= +0.40 and -log10(FDR) ~= 2.8 to 2.9, the only triangles in the panel. All other amino acids cluster below the FDR=0.05 dashed line; no FDR<0.05 depletions in this panel.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d5/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
